name: lakshx-chat
description: LakshX's agent panel — talks to the LakshX Agent Runtime over ACP.

commands.title: LakshX: Enable Remote Access (view and control this chat from your phone)

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with LakshX
walkthroughs.description: Set up your AI provider and open the agent for the first time.

steps.title: Sign in for the free model
steps.description:
Sign in to unlock LakshX's free built-in model — no API key needed.
[Sign In](command:lakshx.login)

steps.title: Open the agent
steps.description:
Open the agent panel and ask it anything about your code.
[Open Agent](command:lakshx.openAgent)

steps.title: Or bring your own key
steps.description:
Prefer your own Anthropic, OpenAI, Azure, or OpenRouter key? Configure it any time.
[Configure Providers](command:lakshx.configureProviders)

lakshx.agent.command.description: Command to launch the LakshX Agent Runtime (ACP over stdio). Empty = auto-detect the bundled runtime.

lakshx.debugExplain.enabled.description: When a debug session stops on an unhandled exception, show a dismissible notification offering to explain it with LakshX. Turning this off does not disable 'LakshX: Explain This Crash' from the command palette — it still works off the most recently captured exception.

lakshx.debugExplain.autoSend.description: Zero-click: automatically send the crash context to LakshX as soon as a debug session stops on an unhandled exception, instead of showing a notification first. Off by default.

lakshx.voice.enabled.description: Show the mic (push-to-talk dictation) button in the chat composer. The button renders regardless of this fork's Electron build — on a stock/unpatched build it fails gracefully with a clear message instead of transcribing, since microphone access in the webview requires LakshX's own patched Electron (docs/research/14-voice-mode.md).

enumDescriptions: Hindi-English code-mixed, the way Indian developers actually talk.
lakshx.explainLanguage.description: Explain errors, plans, diffs, and general responses in this code-mixed register instead of plain English. Code, terminal commands, file paths, and identifiers are never translated or mixed — only the agent's explanatory prose shifts register. Off (plain English) by default.
